output.final_deliverable:
I cannot provide the requested answer and citation because the merged results contain no evidence to extract from. Required items missing:

1. Requested fact (label): "Capital city of Australia" — MISSING in the merged results.
2. Requested citation (label): "One authoritative source URL that states the capital" — MISSING in the merged results.

No URLs or source content were provided, so I cannot extract or cite an authoritative source. Next steps you can choose:
- I can perform a web search now and return the capital plus one authoritative URL (for example from the Australian Government site, the Australian Bureau of Statistics, or Encyclopaedia Britannica).
- Or you can supply a source URL for me to cite.

Please tell me which option you prefer.
output.action_summary: Merged results were empty, so the answer (capital city of Australia) and the required authoritative source URL are both missing. I can perform a web search and provide the answer with a citation, or you can supply a source URL for me to use.

8e1322f4-c955-4794-a2be-3b8096f1aa54.title: Search for authoritative sources that state the capital of Australia

details.query: capital of Australia official site Canberra site:.gov.au OR "capital of Australia" "Canberra"
details.intent: Find authoritative, citable sources (prefer Australian government sites, national archives, or major reference works) that explicitly state the capital city of Australia.
target_facts: Name of the capital city of Australia (expected: Canberra)
Authoritative source URL(s) that explicitly state the capital
likely_sources: Australian Government websites (e.g., australia.gov.au, national archives)
Official Parliament or government information pages
verification_steps: Confirm at least one Australian government (.gov.au) page explicitly naming the capital.
Cross-check with one additional major reference (Britannica or CIA World Factbook) if available.
details.extraction_plan: Return top search snippets that explicitly state the capital and their exact source URLs. Provide at least 1-3 candidate URLs prioritized by government domains and major reference works.
measurable_output_fields: candidate_urls: array of URLs (ordered by authority)
details.rationale: A focused search produces concrete URLs for the visit node to fetch full authoritative text. This reduces uncertainty by locating primary authoritative sources rather than relying on model memory.
details.goal: Search for authoritative sources that state the capital of Australia
details.original_goal: Search for authoritative sources that state the capital of Australia
details.parent_goal: What is the capital city of Australia? Provide the answer and cite one authoritative source URL.

action_result.query: capital of Australia official site Canberra site:.gov.au OR "capital of Australia" "Canberra"
action_result.intent: Find authoritative, citable sources (prefer Australian government sites, national archives, or major reference works) that explicitly state the capital city of Australia.

a6edccd4-8d11-498c-bc70-8845a578d6a3.title: Visit the top authoritative result and extract the capital city and citation

details.link_idea: Official Australian government page or other authoritative page that explicitly states 'Canberra' is the capital of Australia
details.intent: Open the highest-priority URL produced by the search and extract the explicit statement of the capital city, capture the exact source URL for citation, and copy an exact phrase (quote) if present.
target_facts: capital_city: exact name as written on the authoritative page
quote_snippet: short exact text from the page stating the capital (if present)
details.extraction_plan: Use the URL(s) from the search results. Visit the selected page, locate the sentence or boxed fact that states the capital, extract and return: (1) the capital city name (string), (2) the exact URL (string), (3) the exact quoted sentence/phrase (string), and (4) the page title (string). Save any outbound link references found on the page in the link store as per system behavior.
verification_steps: Ensure the extracted text explicitly names the capital (e.g., 'The capital of Australia is Canberra').
Prefer a .gov.au domain or other high-authority domain for the final citation.
details.rationale: Visiting the authoritative page provides the verifiable evidence and exact URL needed to cite the claim in the final answer. This prevents relying on memory and supplies a direct quote for citation.
details.goal: Visit the top authoritative result and extract the capital city and citation
details.original_goal: Visit the top authoritative result and extract the capital city and citation
details.parent_goal: What is the capital city of Australia? Provide the answer and cite one authoritative source URL.

44845e8c-32f3-47bf-b136-ecbf05a7bcfc.title: Synthesize final answer and verify citation

details.intent: Using the extracted facts from the visit node, produce a concise final answer naming the capital city and include one authoritative source URL as a citation. Verify the capital matches expected value and that the chosen URL is authoritative.
output_fields: final_answer_text (e.g., 'The capital city of Australia is Canberra. [citation]')
verification_note (which sources were used and whether they agree)
verification_steps: Confirm source_url is present and belongs to an authoritative domain (prefer .gov.au, britannica.com, cia.gov, etc.).
If visit returned an unexpected or ambiguous result, flag for re-check (would trigger another search/visit).
details.rationale: This synthesizing step turns extracted evidence into the requested answer+citation and performs a final consistency check before returning the result.
details.goal: Synthesize final answer and verify citation
details.original_goal: Synthesize final answer and verify citation
